name: Duo Security
description: Cloud multi-factor authentication and device trust platform. Yields MFA policy configuration, authentication logs, user and device enrolment reports, and Admin API log feeds for SIEM ingestion.

steps.action: Sign in to the Duo Admin Panel and navigate to Policies; capture the Global Policy and any custom policy settings (authentication methods, new user policy, authorised networks) via screenshots
steps.security_note: Use a Read-only administrator account for evidence collection, not an Owner login
steps.audit_note: Capture which applications each custom policy is applied to, so reviewers can verify MFA enforcement coverage

steps.action: Navigate to Reports > Authentication Log and set the date range and filters to cover the collection period (e.g., last 7 days)
steps.audit_note: Ensure no gaps between collection periods

steps.action: Click Export in the upper right of the log display and download the authentication log as CSV or JSON
steps.audit_note: Exported files include additional log fields not shown in the Admin Panel and reflect the applied filters — record the filter set with the evidence

steps.action: Navigate to Users and use the export option to download the user list as CSV for enrolment status evidence; for a summary view, use Reports > Deployment Progress
steps.audit_note: The user export evidences enrolment coverage; flag any users in bypass status for review

steps.action: Export Reports > Administrator Actions for the same period to evidence administrative changes to policies and configuration
Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Verify exports do not contain secrets or personal data beyond what the evidence requires before uploading

L2.title: Duo Scheduled Log Collection via Duo Log Sync

steps.action: Note: the Duo Admin Panel does not offer built-in scheduled or emailed report exports. The closest vendor-supported no-code option is Duo Log Sync (duologsync), Duo's own configuration-driven log collection utility

Have an Owner create an Admin API application via Applications > Application Catalog > Admin API in the Duo Admin Panel, granting only the 'Grant read log' permission, and record the integration key, secret key and API hostname
steps.security_note: Treat the secret key (skey) like a password; grant only 'Grant read log' so the credential cannot modify the Duo account
steps.audit_note: Admin API application creation is itself recorded in Administrator Actions

steps.action: Install duologsync on a hardened collection host and configure config.yml with the API hostname, integration key and secret key, enabling the required endpoints (authentication, administrator actions, telephony)
steps.security_note: Restrict read access to config.yml, which contains the API credentials; run the utility under a dedicated service account

steps.action: Point the output at your log collector or evidence store (TCP, TCP over SSL, or UDP transport; JSON or CEF format) and run duologsync as a scheduled service (e.g., systemd)
steps.audit_note: Duo Log Sync checkpoints its last-known offset, so restarts resume without gaps in the evidence timeline

steps.action: Verify each cycle that logs arrived for the collection period and file the collected output in the evidence repository with generation timestamps

Create a dedicated Admin API application via Applications > Application Catalog > Admin API in the Duo Admin Panel and record the integration key (ikey), secret key (skey) and API hostname (https://api-XXXXXXXX.duosecurity.com)
steps.permissions_required: Owner (only Owners can create or modify Admin API applications)
steps.security_note: Grant least privilege — 'Grant read log' plus 'Grant read information'/'Grant read resource' as needed; never grant write permissions to an evidence-collection credential

steps.action: Implement the collection script to call GET /admin/v2/logs/authentication with mintime/maxtime parameters, signing each request with HMAC-SHA1 using the skey and passing the ikey as the HTTP Basic Auth username
steps.permissions_required: Admin API application with 'Grant read log'
steps.audit_note: The v2 authentication logs endpoint returns Duo-generated, timestamped events; the API serves up to 180 days of history, so daily collection stays well within retention

steps.action: Extend the script to also collect GET /admin/v1/logs/administrator (administrator actions) and GET /admin/v1/users (enrolment and MFA status per user, with limit/offset paging)
steps.audit_note: Pairing authentication logs with the user inventory evidences both MFA operation and enrolment coverage

steps.action: Schedule the script via cron or a CI/CD pipeline to run daily, writing date-stamped outputs to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Store the skey in a secrets manager, never in plaintext configuration files or source control; rotate the credential on a defined schedule

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed collection runs, including HTTP 429 rate-limit handling with retry and backoff
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should be logged and trigger re-collection within SLA

steps.action: Validate collected data against expected volumes — flag anomalies if the daily authentication event count drops below baseline or the user count changes unexpectedly
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Duo Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Run Duo Log Sync as a continuously running service (or use your SIEM's native Duo connector) streaming authentication, administrator action, telephony and Trust Monitor logs into the SIEM. Note: Duo does not offer outbound webhooks for log delivery — API polling via Duo Log Sync or a SIEM connector is the vendor-supported continuous option
steps.permissions_required: Owner to create the Admin API application; SIEM administrator for the collector
steps.security_note: Use TCP over SSL transport to the SIEM collector and restrict access to the credential-bearing configuration

steps.action: Enable Duo Trust Monitor in the Admin Panel to surface anomalous authentication events, and include its security events in the streamed feed
steps.audit_note: Trust Monitor events evidence active monitoring of MFA anomalies, not just log retention

steps.action: Configure collection completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day per log type, ingestion latency, and gap detection using the Duo Log Sync checkpoint offsets
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are the key evidence that the collection process itself is managed and reliable

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks: schema validation of ingested events, duplicate detection, and coverage checks across all Duo-protected applications
Build a collection-health dashboard (events/day, p95 ingestion latency, coverage by application, failed collections) with alerting when any feed stalls or volume drops below baseline
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection health report flagging SLA breaches, credential rotation status and any evidence gaps for review
